headline: WIRobotics Secures Approximately KRW 100 Billion (USD 68 Million) Series B Funding
summary: Global robotics company WIRobotics (Co-CEOs Yeonbaek Lee and Yongjae Kim) announced that it has completed a KRW 95 billion (approximately USD 68 million) Series B funding round. The investment comes approximately two years after the company's Series A financing in March 2024 (KRW 13 billion) and reflects growing confidence in WIRobotics' next-generation robotics technologies and commercialization potential.

content.title: WIRobotics Secures Approximately KRW 100 Billion (USD 68 Million) Series B Funding
content.summary: Global robotics company WIRobotics (Co-CEOs Yeonbaek Lee and Yongjae Kim) announced that it has completed a KRW 95 billion (approximately USD 68 million) Series B funding round. The investment comes approximately two years after the company's Series A financing in March 2024 (KRW 13 billion) and reflects growing confidence in WIRobotics' next-generation robotics technologies and commercialization potential.

thumbnail.caption: Photo Courtesy of WIRobotics: “ALLEX,” WIRobotics’ humanoid robot platform currently under development to achieve human-level manipulation intelligence.

headline: Argentum AI signs $2.5 billion data center deal with cloud, real estate firms
summary: Argentum AI has signed a deal worth around $2.5 billion with ‌cloud gaming firm Boosteroid and real ‌estate company DL Invest Group for a 300-megawatt data ​center in Europe, the artificial intelligence infrastructure provider told Reuters on Friday.  Under the deal, Argentum said it will deploy graphics processing unit ‌infrastructure in a ⁠data center, with the company expected to use tens of thousands of ⁠next-generation GPUs, including Nvidia's Blackwell systems in the future.  The deal will be one of ​the largest ​independent AI infrastructure ​projects in the region. ‌It comes as the tech industry rushes to secure compute capacity to meet soaring demand, leading to the emergence of independent infrastructure providers such as Argentum.

content.title: Argentum AI signs $2.5 billion data center deal with cloud, real estate firms
content.summary: Argentum AI has signed a deal worth around $2.5 billion with ‌cloud gaming firm Boosteroid and real ‌estate company DL Invest Group for a 300-megawatt data ​center in Europe, the artificial intelligence infrastructure provider told Reuters on Friday.  Under the deal, Argentum said it will deploy graphics processing unit ‌infrastructure in a ⁠data center, with the company expected to use tens of thousands of ⁠next-generation GPUs, including Nvidia's Blackwell systems in the future.  The deal will be one of ​the largest ​independent AI infrastructure ​projects in the region. ‌It comes as the tech industry rushes to secure compute capacity to meet soaring demand, leading to the emergence of independent infrastructure providers such as Argentum.

headline: Nvidia Stock’s 7-Day Winning Streak Has Room to Run
summary: Nvidia  stock is finally breaking out in a big way.  It has recorded seven consecutive days of gains, rising 20% over the period, although the stock was down 1% in premarket trading Friday.  UBS  analyst Timothy Arcuri raised his target price on Nvidia to $275 from $245 in a research note previewing the report, maintaining a Buy rating.

headline: Elon Musk Says 'Space Is The Only Way' After Jensen Huang Warns AI Could Soon Need 1,000x More Energy
summary: Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX CEO Elon Musk reaffirmed orbital datacenter goals on Thursday as NVIDIA Corp (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang outlined the growing need for energy to operate AI compute. Space Is The Only Way User @TheChiefNerd posted a clip on X that showed Huang sharing that the amount of energy needed for AI compute exceeded current capacity. “The amount of energy that we need for computing is probably 1,000x more than we currently have,” Huang said. Read Also: Elon Musk Says
